chapter_context.summary: David organizes the relocation of the Ark of the Covenant from Kirjath-jearim; Uzza is struck down for touching the Ark, which is then diverted to Obed-edom's home.

outline.text: David Proposes to Bring the Ark from Kirjath-jearim
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Proposes to Bring the Ark from Kirjath-jearim (13:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: First Journey of the Ark on a New Cart with Joyful Music
outline.explanation: This section focuses on First Journey of the Ark on a New Cart with Joyful Music (13:5-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: Uzza Touches Ark and Dies; Ark Left at House of Obed-edom
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Uzza Touches Ark and Dies; Ark Left at House of Obed-edom (13:9-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: Covenant Lineage
key_themes.description: The meticulous genealogies show that God's plan is continuous and links post-exilic Israel back to creation.

key_themes.title: The Throne of David
key_themes.description: David's rise and worship preparations focus on establishing Jerusalem as the central theological hub of the kingdom.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 13, how does seeing God's faithfulness through history encourage you today?
How does this chapter prompt you to dedicate your personal plans and gifts to the LORD's work?

cultural_historical.title: The Post-Exilic Restoration
cultural_historical.text: Chronicles was written for the returning exiles to rebuild their religious identity, focusing heavily on priestly lineages and temple worship.
